title: kaviria cana subsp. futilis
intro: Kaviria cana subsp. futilis, also known as the Kaviria cana, is a plant species classified as a shrub or small tree native to the tropical forests of Sri Lanka. The medicinal properties of Kaviria cana subsp. futilis are primarily attributed to its bark and leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The plant is known for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to have several health benefits, including the potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant activity. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infused in water to create a medicinal concoction, and in some cases, the bark is used to create a paste that can be applied topically to affected areas.
